key_insight: A Lagrangian, trajectory-based integration scheme can reconstruct viscoelastic polymeric stress from known steady velocity fields by using deformation-gradient history instead of solving the full Eulerian constitutive transport equation.
engineering_relevance: This enables stress quantification directly from experimental or simulated velocity data, reducing computational cost and improving applicability to microfluidic and complex-flow viscoelastic analysis.
method: Compute stress along fluid-element trajectories from deformation-gradient history
Support both FENE-P and Oldroyd-B constitutive models
Apply to canonical flows and channel flows past circular obstacles using simulation and experimental velocity fields
evidence_signal: The reconstructed stress fields agreed with traditional Eulerian reference solutions in both numerical simulations and microfluidic experiments.
watch_item: The approach assumes a known, steady velocity field and requires accurate trajectory/deformation-history reconstruction, which may be sensitive to measurement noise and unsteady flows.
paper_title: Lagrangian evaluation of polymeric stress in viscoelastic fluids
